competitors.name: Omni Calculator
features: Clean, modern UI with step-by-step explanation
Multiple related calculators linked (percentage change, decrease, etc.)
weaknesses: High ad density can be intrusive
Page load is slower due to many widgets and trackers
Explanation, while good, is text-heavy and could be more interactive

competitors.name: Calculator Soup
features: Clear formula display and calculation steps
No frills, fast for quick calculations
weaknesses: Cluttered layout with many unrelated calculator links

competitors.name: RapidTables
features: Includes a percentage decrease calculator on the same page
Part of a vast library of simple tools
weaknesses: Feels like a utility rather than a helpful tool

competitors.name: Percentage Calculator
features: Dedicated domain name for high keyword relevance
weaknesses: No unique value proposition beyond basic calculation

competitors.name: Marshu.com
features: Article-style format with detailed written examples
Encourages user practice with example problems
weaknesses: Calculation tool is buried in text

gaps.gap: Lack of real-world, contextual examples and templates
gaps.implementation: Build a template library next to the calculator: 'Calculate Salary Raise (5% over $50,000)', 'Calculate Price Inflation', 'Calculate Sales Growth Year-over-Year'. Pre-fill the fields with these examples for one-click learning.

gaps.gap: No data visualization for multi-step or compound increases
gaps.implementation: Add an advanced mode that allows users to calculate percentage increase over multiple periods (e.g., 5% annual growth for 5 years). Display the results as an interactive line chart or bar graph showing the growth trajectory.
Design a fully responsive, mobile-first PWA (Progressive Web App). Use large input fields, swipe-able examples, and haptic feedback. Offer an 'Add to Home Screen' prompt for instant app-like access.

Create a 'Business' mode with features like: bulk upload (CSV) of values to calculate increases for a list, generate a shareable report (PDF/PNG) of the calculation with a company logo, and API access for developers.

gaps.gap: No integration with common productivity platforms
gaps.implementation: Develop lightweight extensions/add-ons for Google Sheets, Microsoft Excel, or Notion. Allow users to insert percentage increase calculations directly into their workflows without leaving their document.

recommended_positioning: The contextual percentage calculator that turns simple math into actionable insights for students, professionals, and businesses.
